legend.provider: Color identifies the provider or managed platform used by each capability.

items.label: Edge Entry
items.summary: CloudFront is ready as the public capability boundary.

items.label: Orders API
items.summary: API Gateway has high readiness and strong implementation confidence.

items.label: Billing Worker
items.summary: Lambda worker is capable but needs idempotency hardening before launch.

items.label: Ledger Store
items.summary: RDS migration is blocked on retention and failover signoff.

items.label: Event Bus
items.summary: EventBridge covers domain event routing with acceptable operational confidence.

items.label: Retry Queue
items.summary: SQS retry handling exists, but dead-letter alarms need ownership.

items.label: Audit Archive
items.summary: S3 archive capability is ready and covered by retention policy tests.

items.label: Secret Rotation
items.summary: Secrets Manager rotation is the highest risk gap in the capability map.

items.label: Release Gate
items.summary: Jenkins deployment gate is stable enough for controlled rollout.

visual.goal: Show which cloud capabilities are ready, risky, or blocked before launch.

narrative_steps.title: Readiness hotspots
narrative_steps.summary: Start with the high-impact API, database, and security capabilities.

narrative_steps.title: Supporting platform
narrative_steps.summary: Then compare eventing, storage, queueing, and release support.

annotations.label: Migration blocker
annotations.summary: Retention and failover signoff are required before launch.

annotations.label: Risk gap
annotations.summary: Rotation coverage is below the launch threshold.
